California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Mary M. Miyaslina 
15920 Youngwood 
Whittier, CA 
90603 
Dear Ms. Miyaslina: 
July 6, 1988 
Re: 
Enclosed Copy of Advice 
Regarding Slate Mailers 
Our File No. A-88-186 
The enclosed letter is sent to you because records on file 
with the Secretary of State's Office indicate that you are an 
officer with, or a participant in, a registered slate mailer 
organization. 
We want to be sure that you are aware of the 
Commission's interpretation of the requirements regarding 
disclaimers on slate mailercs. 
Please review the enclosed letter. 
We believe that it is 
self-explanatory. 
However, if you have questions you may reach 
me at (916) 322-5901. 
Enclosure 
Letter No. A-88-186 
Sincerely, 
Diane M. Griffiths 
General Counsel

Fair Political

Daniel H. Lowenstein 
UCLA Law School 
405 Hilgard 
Los Angeles, CA 
90024 
Dear Mr. Lowenstein: 
May 19, 1988 
Re: 
88-186 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform 
Act was received on May 18, 1988 by the Fair Political 
Practices Commission. 
If you have any questions about your 
advice request, you may contact Robert Leidigh, an attorney in 
the Legal.Division, directly at (916) 322-5901. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, 
or more information is needed, you should expect a response 
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written 
advice. 
If more information is needed, the person assigned to 
prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to 
advise you as to information needed. 
If your request is for 
informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we can. 
(See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 
18329) .) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
DMG:plh 
Very truly yours, 
( 
Diane M. Griffiths 
General Counsel

sections.question_synthetic: What are the FPPC's requirements regarding disclaimers on slate mailers that officers and participants in registered slate mailer organizations should be aware of?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC is providing guidance on disclaimer requirements for slate mailers to officers and participants in registered slate mailer organizations, with the enclosed letter containing the specific interpretation and requirements.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is a mass mailing of transmittal letters without traditional Q&A sections; the substantive advice letter (A-88-186) is referenced as enclosed but not fully included in the provided text. Multiple recipients identified (13+). Letters follow identical template structure.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What are the FPPC's requirements regarding disclaimers on slate mailers that officers and participants in registered slate mailer organizations should be aware of?

CONCLUSION: The FPPC is providing guidance on disclaimer requirements for slate mailers to officers and participants in registered slate mailer organizations, with the enclosed letter containing the specific interpretation and requirements.

Santell 336 Barranca west covina, CA 91791 Dear Mr. Santell: July 6, 1988 Re: Enclosed Copy of Advice Regarding Slate Mailers Our File No. A-88-186 The enclosed letter is sent to you because records on file with the Secretary of State's Office indicate that you are an officer with, or a participant in, a registered slate mailer organization. We want to be sure that you are aware of the Commission's interpretation of the requirements regarding disclaimers on slate mailers. Please review the enclosed letter. We believe that it is self-explanatory. However, if you have questions you may reach me at (916) 322-5901. Enclosure Letter No. A-88-186 Sincerely, Diane M. embedding.summary: This document consists of multiple identical transmittal letters dated July 6, 1988, sent by FPPC General Counsel Diane M. Griffiths to various slate mailer organization officers and participants. Each letter encloses advice (Letter No. A-88-186) regarding the FPPC's interpretation of disclaimer requirements for slate mailers.
